constraints.expression: consumer routes severity to the appropriate channel: 'info' → digest, 'warn' → Telegram, 'critical' → email, 'panic' → SMS + auto-execute (consumer-side policy per ADR-010)
constraints.message: Escalation severity-to-channel routing is consumer-side. The schema declares the four severity literals; the consumer dispatches to the appropriate channel.
constraints.evaluation_note: Severity-to-channel routing is consumer policy per ADR-010. The schema validates that severity is one of {info, warn, critical, panic}; the channel mapping (digest, Telegram, email, SMS, auto-execute) is the consumer's responsibility. Runtime-deferred classification surfaces the obligation in the UnverifiedObligationsManifest so consumers building escalation pipelines see the routing-policy hook.

constraints.expression: consumer ensures idempotency_key uniqueness per (cluster_id, escalation_id) tuple to prevent duplicate-fire on retry; replay of the same idempotency_key MUST produce identical channel-side action
constraints.message: Idempotency invariant: the same idempotency_key emitted twice MUST be treated as the same escalation, not duplicated.
constraints.evaluation_note: Cross-record idempotency. The schema enforces 64-hex format at the structural layer; the consumer-side persistence layer maintains the dedup index. Runtime-deferred because the library cannot enforce cross-record invariants from a single envelope.
